final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original museum-style Chinese infographic illustration showcasing traditional Chinese ethnic minority costume (中国少数民族服饰), formatted as a scholarly garment atlas panel in the style of a national museum exhibition board.

Style/medium: needs a museum exhibition board / historical garment atlas aesthetic — exact ethnic group selection, garment system, and illustrative execution are yours
Composition: must follow this fixed layout:
  - Top: 中文主标题 + 副标题 + 导语 (main title, subtitle, introductory text in simplified Chinese)
  - Left: structural breakdown zone with Chinese leader-line annotations on key garment components, with close-up detail insets
  - Upper right: materials / craftsmanship / texture zone showing realistic fabric swatches with Chinese captions
  - Middle right: patterns / colors / cultural meaning zone showing main color palette, pattern samples, and cultural interpretation in Chinese
  - Bottom: dressing sequence / construction flowchart + core characteristics summary in Chinese
Subject: must include a full-body standing figure (realistic human, not illustrated/anime) as the central subject if the chosen ethnic costume suits figure display; otherwise use a central structural exploded diagram — either way the full Chinese infographic structure must be preserved
Scene/backdrop: must use a paper-texture background — 米白、绢纸白 or 浅茶色 tone; restrained, archival, collectible feel
Lighting/mood: needs a neutral, evenly-lit scholarly atmosphere — exact light treatment is yours
Color palette: needs a historically grounded, culturally accurate palette drawn from the chosen ethnic costume — exact color values and swatches are yours
Materials/textures: must include realistic fabric texture swatches (e.g., embroidery, weave, dye patterns) rendered with material fidelity — exact surface rendering is yours
Text (verbatim): must include all annotations, labels, titles, captions, flowchart text, and cultural explanations written in 简体中文 (Simplified Chinese) — no English, no pinyin, no garbled characters, no typos; all text must be legible, neat, and correctly structured
Constraints: must preserve accurate garment structure, authentic material differentiation, cultural annotation accuracy, and atlas-quality visual hierarchy; auto-select the most representative and visually rich ethnic minority costume system; all Chinese text must be clear and readable
Avoid: poster aesthetic, photo-studio aesthetic, e-commerce aesthetic, anime/illustration aesthetic, cosplay aesthetic, chaotic annotations, incorrect garment structure, blurry or garbled text, fake-looking materials, excessive decorative elements
